free_text: Determination of unbound states in 35S from neutron
total and capture cross-section measurements on 34S
VM: updated to new date formats, lower case
BP: Updated entry, added doi, digitized
It weighed 14.96 g and had a
thickness of 0.0811 atoms of 34S per barn.
9-m flight path for total cross section.
Data received on magnetic tape, J.Harvey,
NE225. 6Li glass scintillator detector to measure flux.
78.2-m flight path for capture cross section.
. sample dependent and independent beam induced
measured total energy loss corrected for a 3.4% energy
data analyzed with multi-level code SAMMY. Capture
data analyzed with code LSFIT. Resonance parameters
based on capture data below 116 keV, on combined
transmission and capture data and on transmission
data alone at higher energies. Resonance energies
assumed for those resonances for which it was not
possible to determine the width from capture data.
Channel radius assumed to be 4.37 fermi.
ratio and asymmetry in the resonance shape
(l,J) assignment is based on phase shift trends
l assignment is based on reduced width
Radiation widths corrected for detector sensitivity
to prompt neutrons detected from the sample.
C(en) is an energy dependent factor ranging from
statistical uncertainty combined in quadrature with
10% correction for the missing levels Error for the l
Data units corrected for capture width.
Strength function data corrected. Data summed over
